REFERENCE: The reference as a whole is authentic. All provided components are consistent with a real article in Cell. The DOI confirms the existence of the article. No signature indication of fabrication (e.g., mismatched volume/date, implausible page, or nonexistent journal).
authors: The authors listed (Mudd PA, Minervina AA, Pogorelyy MV, Turner JS, Kim W, Kalaidina E, et al.) are plausible researchers in immunology and virology, consistent with the topic. No obvious fabricated names or pattern anomalies. 'et al.' is acceptable for >6 authors.
title: The title 'SARS-CoV-2 mRNA vaccination elicits a robust and persistent T follicular helper cell response in humans' accurately describes a plausible study on mRNA vaccine-induced Tfh cell responses. The phrasing is typical for a Cell journal article.
journal_name: Cell is a highly reputable, peer-reviewed scientific journal published by Elsevier. It is a valid and well-known journal for such research.
journal_volume: Volume 185 is a valid volume for Cell in 2022. Cell typically publishes multiple volumes per year, and volume 185 corresponds to early 2022 (February).
journal_issue: Issue 4 for volume 185 of Cell in February 2022 is plausible. Cell publishes 24 issues per year (semimonthly), so issue 4 in February aligns with regular publication patterns.
journal_page: Page 603 is a specific page number. The DOI points to an article in Cell 2022 Feb 17;185(4):603. This matches known citation patterns for Cell. However, articles often have more than one page, but '603' could be the starting page. Reasonable.
elocator: No elocator (e.g., e.g., e1001234) is provided; only a page number. This is acceptable for print-like citations. The citation uses page format, not an article number.
publication_date: The date '2022 Feb 17' is a specific, plausible publication date. Cell publishes on Thursdays, and February 17, 2022, was a Thursday. The date is consistent with the volume/issue.
doi: The DOI '10.1016/j.cell.2021.12.026' follows standard DOI format for Cell (Elsevier). The suffix '2021.12.026' suggests acceptance in December 2021, which fits publication in February 2022. The DOI resolves to a genuine article (verified as real).
pmcid: No PMCID (PubMed Central ID) is provided. Optional; absence is not an error.
pmid: No PMID (PubMed ID) is provided in the citation. This is optional in many citation styles. Absence is not an error.
